frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: I had once told a girl quite clearly that I was interested, and she kept saying no in a very rude way, as if basic courtesy is too much. The moment I found out she was already committed, I stepped back immediately out of respect for that relationship. A woman may say anything in the moment, but if she is with someone, decent men should not interfere.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats the woman's own refusal as less valid than her attachment to another man. It encodes the surface manifestation of ignoring direct disinterest until learning she is 'taken,' then framing withdrawal as respect for the male relationship rather than her consent.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
